session.name: 1.1.1 verification session that pivoted to shipping 1.2.0 to GitHub (drop 1.1)

goal.original: Build the comprehensive public 1.2.0 changelog for the Thursday GitHub release, aggregating 1.1.0 + 1.1.1 into 1.2.0 (1.1 is being skipped publicly).
goal.summary: Diagnosed the problem fully; NOT started implementing (deliberately deferred to a fresh session - the source session was ~300k tokens). The 1.2.0 changelog is unbuilt and its sources are out of sync.

completed: Diagnosed: GitHub public jumps 1.0.1 -> 1.2.0; 1.1.0 + 1.1.1 never shipped publicly.
Confirmed with user this is NOT simple concat - bug-fix overlap between 1.1 and 1.2 must be deduped.
pending: Rebuild RELEASE_NOTES['1.2.0'] in js/data/releaseNotes.js as ONE complete curated entry (fold UNRELEASED + 1.1.0 + master's 1.1.1, dedup overlapping fixes, curate for first-time users).
Clear docs/releases/UNRELEASED.md back to its header comment.
Decide whether to also ship the 18+ modal backdrop fix (shell.js:293-302 sequence).

task_card.title: Aggregate 1.1.0 + 1.1.1 + UNRELEASED into the public 1.2.0 changelog

allowed_actions: implement (after checking with the agent wrapping up 1.2 verifications - shared tree)

completed: Full diagnosis captured in MPI-327 card description and this handoff.
pending: The actual curated 1.2.0 changelog must be authored in the fresh session.
Related bug MPI-328 (remote model-check fail-open) filed separately for the 1.2 wrap-up agent to judge.

next_action.description: Read docs/releases/UNRELEASED.md (the real 1.2.0 content), RELEASE_NOTES['1.2.0'] + ['1.1.0'] in js/data/releaseNotes.js, and `git show master:js/data/releaseNotes.js` for the 1.1.1 entry. Then rebuild RELEASE_NOTES['1.2.0'] as one curated entry that folds all three, deduping bug-fix overlap (the download queue: 1.1 introduced it, 1.2's MPI-276 refactor reworked it - do not double-list or contradict) and phrasing for a first-time GitHub user (intros not refinements). Update docs/releases/2026-07-09-v1.2.0.md and clear UNRELEASED.md. FIRST confirm the agent wrapping up 1.2 is not also editing these files.

key_decisions: Ship 1.2.0 to GitHub Thursday and abandon 1.1 entirely (zero Patreons, low traction, weak initial models; KREA2/Chroma in 1.2 are the draw).
Changelog is a DATA merge into RELEASE_NOTES['1.2.0'], not the minor-based aggregation code idea - that code cannot cross 1.1 -> 1.2 (different minor).
Defer the changelog build to a fresh session because this one hit ~300k tokens and the dedup needs careful judgment.
constraints: SOURCE 1: RELEASE_NOTES['1.2.0'] (js/data/releaseNotes.js) is a STALE STUB - only 'Chroma Hyper' + 'Chroma Flash faster'; neither is in UNRELEASED. Do not treat it as the base.

SOURCE 2: docs/releases/UNRELEASED.md = the REAL 1.2.0 content (~22 features incl KREA2, KREA2 NSFW, Qwen Image Edit, Boogu Image Edit, Remove Background, Export GIF, frame-accurate playback, RunPod-unlock, mascot, Settings redesign + ~28 fixes), never folded.

SOURCE 3: RELEASE_NOTES['1.1.0'] (RunPod, Model Library, LTX 2.3, NVIDIA PiD, Wan 2.2 5B, Chroma Flash intro) + the 1.1.1 entry which exists ONLY on master (git show master:js/data/releaseNotes.js -> 3 fixes).
DEDUP is the hard part: bugs fixed in 1.1 re-fixed/refactored in 1.2 (download queue especially) must not double-list or contradict.
Curate for a first-time GitHub user: intros not refinements ('Chroma Flash IS...' not '...is faster'; 'RunPod remote engine' not 'toggle moved').
SHARED TREE: another agent is wrapping up 1.2 verifications - do not double-edit releaseNotes.js / UNRELEASED.md without checking.
Out of scope for Thursday: getAggregatedReleaseNotes(major.minor) helper for future 1.2.x patch accumulation (MpiChangelogDialog reads getReleaseNotes(APP_VERSION) exact-key today).

Optional to include: 18+ adult-content modal (MpiOkCancel) stacks over the changelog with no dim backdrop - z-index floor collapse at MpiModal.js:95; fix by sequencing at shell.js:293-302 (18+ first, changelog after Continue).

resume_prompt: Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/f7677602-e881-4c74-b412-666604cf366b.json and use mpi-continue on task MPI-327. You are building the public 1.2.0 changelog for a Thursday GitHub release - GitHub skips 1.1 entirely (1.1.0 + 1.1.1 never shipped publicly), so their content must fold into RELEASE_NOTES['1.2.0']. Three sources are out of sync: the RELEASE_NOTES['1.2.0'] entry in js/data/releaseNotes.js is a stale stub (only Chroma Hyper + 'Chroma Flash faster'); docs/releases/UNRELEASED.md holds the REAL 1.2.0 content (KREA2, Qwen Edit, Boogu Edit, Remove Background, Export GIF, frame-accurate playback, RunPod-unlock, ~22 features + ~28 fixes) that was never folded; and RELEASE_NOTES['1.1.0'] plus the 1.1.1 entry that lives ONLY on master (git show master:js/data/releaseNotes.js) hold the Patreon-only content. Rebuild RELEASE_NOTES['1.2.0'] as ONE complete curated entry that folds all three - this is NOT a blind concat: bugs fixed in 1.1 were re-fixed or refactored in 1.2 (the download queue especially - 1.1 introduced it, 1.2's MPI-276 refactor reworked it), so dedup and reconcile overlapping fix items, and phrase everything for a first-time GitHub user (intros, not refinements). Then update docs/releases/2026-07-09-v1.2.0.md and clear UNRELEASED.md back to its header. IMPORTANT: another agent is wrapping up 1.2 verifications on the shared tree - confirm they are not also editing releaseNotes.js or UNRELEASED.md before you write. Separately, bug MPI-328 (remote model-check fail-open) was filed for that wrap-up agent to judge.
